comments.author: Progress
comments.body:
纠正前一条 comment：Wuji Glove 不是 MANUS。

从 docs.wuji.tech/docs/en/wuji-glove/latest/ 拉到的真实信息：
- Wuji Glove 是 Shenzhen Wuji Technology 的自研数据手套（tactile + EMF poses + IMU），不是 MANUS。
- 物理连接：glove → USB-C cable → Wuji USB-C-to-Ethernet power adapter (DC powered) → Ethernet → host RJ45。从主机看是一个普通的有线网卡 + 网络端点；lsusb 不会出现新设备。
- Default IP: 左手 192.168.1.100 / 右手 192.168.1.101，host 同段（如 192.168.1.10/24, mask 255.255.255.0）。
- SDK: pip install wuji-sdk (v0.10.0, github.com/wuji-technology/wuji-sdk)。入口 from wuji_sdk import SdkManager; m.scan()+m.connect(sn=..., device_name='glove')。
- Data streams: glove.tactile() 24×32, .tactile_zones(), .tactile_point_cloud(), .emf_poses(), .tip_poses(), .hand_joint_angles() 21-DoF, .hand_skeleton() 21 MediaPipe, .imu_palm(); manager.tf_static()/tf()。时间戳 UTC μs，SDK 自动同步主机时钟。
- 可选 GUI: wuji-studio v2026.5.18 (apt 安装 .deb)。

当前现场状态 (2026-05-22 16:13)：
- Boris 把右手 glove 的 Wuji adapter 接到了 Arm A 原本的 RJ45 (enp58s0)。Arm A 在此期间不可用。
- enp58s0 carrier=0；DC 电源 + USB-C 方向均报告正确；可能还要看 glove 腕带 LED 是否进入 'Cyan breathing (4s)' 待机态。
- 已发 sudo bundle 给 Boris 配 192.168.1.10/24 on enp58s0 并 ping 192.168.1.101。

下一步: link up → pip install wuji-sdk → 写 smoke 脚本拉一帧 tactile/joint_angles 作为 verifier。状态保持 doing。

Verified facts:
- 网络: enp58s0 上 192.168.1.10/24, ping 192.168.1.101 → 4/4 RTT 0.12-0.20ms (Arm A 原 RJ45 复用)
- 设备发现: sn=WG1KA02260410009 ip=192.168.1.101:50001 transport=UDP (UDP discovery port 50000, data port 50001)
- 设备类型: WG110/WG1K G-Board H743, Right hand, 24×32 tactile matrix
- SDK: pip install wuji-sdk == 0.10.0 (装入 ~/workspace/wuji_wrapper/.venv)

Anomaly (硬件 / 配置, 非阻塞):

- ✓ 网络  - ✓ 时间戳  - ✓ 高频输入流(7 streams 全部活跃)

comments.author: Progress
comments.body:
Continuing the TODO. MANUS-side blocker confirmed concrete + reproducible; VIVE-side scaffolded offline.

== MANUS hardware E2E (still blocked) ==

Reproduced the libusb permission failure with hard evidence:
- log:   ~/.claude/jobs/manus_vive/probe_segfault.log  sha256=d2715e96c542ff66cf6df2f9101b511110efe9af3ca29b1b204cfbc304bde2d7
- cmd:   cd ~/workspace/manus_wrapper && python3 scripts/probe_dongle.py
- exit:  139 (SIGSEGV) — libManusSDK_Integrated.so segfaults attempting libusb_open on /dev/bus/usb/003/013 (mode 0664 root:root, boris in plugdev but device not group-rw)
- root cause: udev rule '99-manus-libusb.rules' (SUBSYSTEM==usb, ATTR{idVendor}==3325, MODE=0666) is not installed in /etc/udev/rules.d/; no system-managed alternative grants non-root access to vid 0x3325 devices.

manus_wrapper offline tests still PASS (26/26) so the wrapper code itself is healthy:
- cd ~/workspace/manus_wrapper && python3 -m pytest tests/

== VIVE Tracker scaffold (new artifact, offline-verifiable) ==

New sibling repo at ~/workspace/vive_wrapper/ (mirrors manus_wrapper / wuji_glove_wrapper layout).
- Initial commit: 1aa1dd2aeb7a5ce3ac8058248f3a515771667f07 (root commit, 12 files, 886 insertions)
- Author: boris <lv.robotics.lab@gmail.com>, no co-author trailer (per memory).

Structure:
  src/vive_wrapper/{tracker.py, config.py, cli.py, __init__.py}
  scripts/{probe_module.py, list_trackers.py}
  examples/openvr_input.yaml
  tests/test_offline.py (10 tests)
  docs/manus_vive_integration.md (unified MANUS+VIVE+camera+robot design)

OpenVRTrackerWrapper is adapted from wuji-hand-teleop's openvr_input/openvr_tracker_wrapper.py with the ROS2 dependency stripped and explicit dual timestamps (CLOCK_MONOTONIC_RAW ns + UTC μs) added so it composes with wuji_glove_wrapper's UTC-μs time base. Coordinate-correction matrices (CHEST / LEFT_WRIST / RIGHT_WRIST) preserved BIT-EXACTLY from upstream — guarded by test_correction_matrices_bitwise_match_upstream so any existing retargeting recipe / recorded trajectory remains valid.

Verifier runs (logs in ~/.claude/jobs/manus_vive/):
- vive_tests.log              sha256=89e2eeda771e06ef84f59be6d2ee8d7f5ab5be6b7d3794c851cdfdbf82ef9077
  cmd: cd ~/workspace/vive_wrapper && .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/ -v
  result: 10 passed in 0.09s
- vive_probe.log              sha256=90320023804dfb3c2bb7cbebdc3c2aa13bba1e77d786d11eff8b5b87e89c74b6
  cmd: .venv/bin/python scripts/probe_module.py
  result: openvr 2.12.1401 loads, all 8 required symbols present (init, shutdown, VRSystem, VRApplication_Other, TrackedDeviceClass_GenericTracker, k_unMaxTrackedDeviceCount, Prop_SerialNumber_String, TrackingUniverseStanding)
- vive_list_no_steamvr.log    sha256=591b2e3cfe46bc6561eb2fff865623890c4169c062f4eacfc32787d7454d80dd
  cmd: .venv/bin/python scripts/list_trackers.py
  result: clean ViveError 'openvr.init failed — is SteamVR running?' (concrete proof that wrapper fails-fast when SteamVR is not running, and that SteamVR is in fact not installed: ~/.steam, ~/.local/share/Steam both absent; no /etc/udev/rules.d/*vive* or SteamVR libraries on PATH)

Env: uv 0.11.7 venv with python 3.10.12, deps openvr==2.12.1401, numpy==2.2.6, pyyaml==6.0.3, pytest==9.0.3.

== Unified integration design doc ==

docs/manus_vive_integration.md (sha256=5681c72388893beaf55ccff7db3ab6790a1a745ce4a0ea8b0e8e05edada63443) covers all four task-description deliverables on paper, ready to execute once hardware is unblocked:
  1. Stream inventory + time-sync invariants (UTC μs join key, <1ms drift bound, 1s heartbeat markers)
  2. Extrinsic calibration (T_world_vive via hand-eye AX=YB on Franka, T_wrist_manus_root via printed jig, T_world_camera reused from existing infra)
  3. Recording format (per-stream Parquet keyed on wall_us, optional ros2 bag)
  4. Retargeting validation (replay manus fingers -> wuji-retargeting, replay vive_wrist -> Franka EE, compare against franka_state; per-session report.html with p95 thresholds)

== What I still need from a human (sudo bundle) ==

Both hardware paths require root. Single sudo session needed:

  # (1) MANUS libusb access — replug dongle after
  sudo cp ~/workspace/manus_wrapper/third_party/99-manus-libusb.rules /etc/udev/rules.d/99-manus-libusb.rules
  sudo udevadm control --reload-rules && sudo udevadm trigger
  # then physically unplug + replug the MANUS Pro dongle (3325:0049)

  # (2) SteamVR install — needs Steam client first
  # easiest path on Ubuntu 22.04:
  sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y steam
  # then run 'steam' as user boris, sign in, install 'SteamVR' from the Tools menu
  # then in SteamVR pair each VIVE Tracker, note its LHR-XXXXXXXX serial

After both: rerun the two verifiers above; the MANUS probe should report dongle id, and vive-list should print serial(s).

Status: leaving as needs_user/blocked (hardware + sudo required, not work I can do under this worker's policy). Concrete deliverables committed: vive_wrapper scaffold @ 1aa1dd2, integration design doc, four reproducible verifier logs with sha256.

comments.author: Progress
comments.body:
Update: udev blocker is resolved (local Codex host did the sudo install). Continued with non-hardware integration work; full MANUS+VIVE fusion adapter now implemented and offline-verified.

== Resolved (no longer blocking) ==

- udev rule installed: /etc/udev/rules.d/99-manus-libusb.rules present (lsof confirmed)
- MANUS dongle now /dev/bus/usb/003/013 crw-rw-rw- (was 0664 root:root)

    * CoreSdk_SetRawSkeletonHandMotion moved POST CoreSdk_ConnectToHost (was segfaulting before)
    * CoreSdk_GetNumberOfDongles / GetNumberOfAvailableGloves return [] on FunctionNotAvailable (CoreLite-host)
- python3 scripts/probe_dongle.py: no more segfault. CoreLite starts all services cleanly (Callbacks, SkeletonService, Coordinator, Devices, Gestures, Calibration, Telemetry), library bridges HIDAPIBridge + EmbeddedBridge load, hot-plug callback thread starts. probe exits 2 only because dongle-enum API is FunctionNotAvailable on CoreLite — not a permission problem.
- manus_wrapper pytest: 26/26 pass

Evidence (sha256 in ~/.claude/jobs/manus_vive/):
  probe_corelite_connect.log    4006572384f22b8d26e3060f162e0a0d4c2b1cb01df21dc06e8d7adcc05b5598
  list_gloves.log               0f3c03c57f1302966a9de776d50fc3474017c679d1fb3769005199f864e13f95

== New deliverable: vive_wrapper @ 979b19c9acaf4bfb08766e4b55359cf9d6458f78 ==

~/workspace/vive_wrapper/ now has both the VIVE adapter AND the fused MANUS+VIVE composition layer described in the task. Two commits:
  1aa1dd2 Initial scaffold for VIVE Tracker integration in MANUS+VIVE input stack
  979b19c Add fused MANUS+VIVE adapter and offline demo

New module: vive_wrapper.fused (src/vive_wrapper/fused.py, sha256=4023ccc63d363f06faf74448c9e6b3d0f8338d1e5a154543710394ddb719096a)

- HandWristCalibration(T_world_vive, T_wrist_manus_root): the two 4x4 transforms calibrated once per platform (see docs/manus_vive_integration.md §3).
- fuse(side, manus_frame, wrist_frame, calibration, skew_max_us=50_000): pure function composing world ← T_world_vive @ T_vive_wrist @ T_wrist_manus_root, transforming MediaPipe-21 keypoints into world, enforcing a configurable skew bound, returning a FusedHandFrame{ side, keypoints_world, wrist_pose_world, wall_us, manus_wall_us, vive_wall_us, skew_us, valid, notes }.
- ManusFrameLike / WristFrameLike: runtime-checkable Protocols. manus_wrapper.GloveFrame (with .to_mediapipe() and .publish_time_ns) plugs in without any import-time coupling.
- StubManusFrame / StubWristFrame: lightweight stand-ins for CI + retargeting validation.

Tests (offline, no hardware, no SteamVR):
  tests/test_fused.py    (sha256=77e1c0175b1a730a299b492f95e4763732c4260f6ff90245cf3547dc09346f50)
  tests/test_offline.py  (sha256=1483ef417a8ea9abf522d815821a122bd541d451485aca624d5ae0a28f38e801)
  Verifier run log: ~/.claude/jobs/manus_vive/vive_tests_v2.log sha256=66852f5df5d9a1128130a903d1471d80f7fbb83ae461303154f09a1198e1021b
  Result: 22 passed in 0.14s
  Coverage: identity passthrough, world translation, z-axis rotation, skew rejection (60ms > 50ms bound), skew acceptance (10ms within bound), max-wall-us aggregation, shape-mismatch reporting, Protocol recognition of stubs, error notes propagation.

Offline E2E demo: examples/fused_demo.py
  Verifier run log: ~/.claude/jobs/manus_vive/vive_fused_demo.log sha256=e0a64cf949b2f71cab9a9e2f4dc7f3a49f1755b85c530708b2574fd3c3ae3765
  Asserts wrist world position = T_world_vive @ T_vive_wrist origin = (0.5, 1.0, 1.2). Pass.

Updated docs: docs/manus_vive_integration.md (sha256=874ed5358df13e1067f5fc72443bba6435055ece1c2cda0406a7c33857108a99) — design now references the actual code module + reflects current hw state.

== Remaining (pure hardware, no sudo needed from a worker) ==

1) MANUS glove actual streaming: power on the glove(s), pair with the dongle in the MANUS Core 3 desktop GUI (or wait for the dongle's pairing window). CoreLite host-level connect is already verified; once a glove appears the skeleton callback in manus_wrapper.sdk will produce frames. After that, examples/quickstart.py in manus_wrapper should print MediaPipe-21 keypoints.

2) VIVE Tracker hardware path: needs Steam + SteamVR install (one-time), plus physical VIVE Tracker(s) + base station(s):
   - install Steam: 'sudo apt install steam' (or .deb from store.steampowered.com)
   - launch Steam → Tools → SteamVR → install
   - plug in VIVE wireless dongle (Bluetooth-flavoured Valve dongle), power on tracker(s), pair in SteamVR's Devices menu, record the LHR-XXXXXXXX serials into examples/openvr_input.yaml
   - then: cd ~/workspace/vive_wrapper && .venv/bin/python scripts/list_trackers.py — should list every paired tracker. Last verified failure (SteamVR not running) at ~/.claude/jobs/manus_vive/vive_list_no_steamvr.log sha=591b2e3cfe46bc6561eb2fff865623890c4169c062f4eacfc32787d7454d80dd.

The integration adapter, design doc, and offline verification cover the four task-description deliverables (time-sync, calibration, recording format, retargeting validation interfaces). tasks.title: 评估主臂重力补偿 / FACTR 力反馈
tasks.description: Gello is passive, so software cannot create true gravity compensation on the master arm. Evaluate mechanical counterbalance, damping / brake, or FACTR-style bilateral force-feedback teleoperation to reduce free-fall / collision risk for Franka control.

comments.author: Progress
comments.body: Core issue is master-arm gravity compensation, not just force feedback. Gello is passive, so software can only filter, limit, or stop follower motion; it cannot hold the master arm up. Treat the 12k FACTR-style device as one benchmark, and ask Yuhang to compare it with mechanical counterbalance, damping, brake, or cheaper bilateral-force options.

comments.author: Progress
comments.body:
FACTR / master-arm gravity comp 调研更新 (2026-05-22)

Repo 现状 (franka_wrapper/gello/factr/):
- gravity_compensation.py: 完整 Pinocchio RNEA + null-space + joint-limit + stiction-dither 实现, 走 Dynamixel current-control。架构正确, 即拿即用的前提是 current-mode-capable 伺服 + 对应 URDF + yaml 配置。
- run_yam_gello_gravity_comp.py / yam_gello_factr_agent.py: 仅适配 6-DOF YAM_GELLO (XM430-W210 在 joint 2,3; XC330-T288 其余), gravity gain 默认 0.4 (有意欠补偿)。
- 引用的 configs/yam_gello_factr_{sim,hw}.yaml 在仓库里**缺失**。
- factr/urdf/ 只有 yam_active_gello/robot.urdf, **没有 7-DOF Franka-Gello URDF**。
- 不是真正双向力反馈: 只有 leader 端 gravity comp + leader→follower 位置 mirror, 没有 Franka external_torque 回传到 leader 关节的通路。

与本实验室主臂(Franka FR3 + 7-DOF Gello)的差距:
- gello_agent.py 里有 7-DOF Gello 注册 (port FT3M9NVB, joint_ids 1-7, gripper id 8), 但 servo 型号未在 factr 配置内记录, 需要硬件确认。
- 若肩/肘关节是 XC330_T288 (~0.9 Nm stall), 软件 GC 顶不动 Franka-Gello 的重量; 必须 XM430-W350 (~4 Nm) 及以上才有意义。

方案对比 (按"防自由落体/碰撞风险"目标):

A. 软件 GC (现 FACTR 代码 → 7-DOF Franka-Gello)
   成本: 0-300 USD (视是否需要升级 joint 2/3/4 伺服)
   工作量: 1-2 周 (URDF + yaml + 调试)
   缺陷: 欠补偿配置下仍有残留下垂; gain=1.0 + URDF 质量不准会"游泳"。
   适合: 第一步, 前提是伺服扭矩够。

B. 机械配重/弹簧
   成本: 50-300 USD
   工作量: 2-4 周 CAD + 加工 + 调试
   优点: 无源安全, 断电也不掉; 不依赖软件。
   缺陷: 单配置最优, 改变 payload 就要重调。
   建议: 与其它方案叠加做被动底盘。

C. 电磁刹车 / 阻尼
   成本: 50-150 USD/关节 × 6-7
   工作量: 1-2 周接线安装
   定位: 在操作员松手 / 死人开关触发时锁住主臂; 不解决主动 GC, 只防"放手就坠"。
   建议: 与 A 或 B 配合上, 主要锁肩/肘 (joint 2,3,4)。

D. 12k FACTR-style 商用双向设备
   成本: ~12,000 USD + lead time
   缺陷: 厂商绑定; 机构通常不对应 Franka FR3, 需要重做 mapping; 与现有 Gello 重复投资。
   建议: 只在双向力反馈是硬需求且预算充足时考虑, 不是首选。

E. 在现 Gello 上加廉价双向 FF (扩 FACTR 代码读 libfranka O_F_ext_K / tau_J_d 注入 leader current)
   成本: 软件 0, 必要时升级伺服 ~300 USD
   工作量: 1-2 周
   缺陷: Dynamixel 扭矩上限决定可渲染力上限 (~3 Nm), 接触刚性物体时会饱和; 增益不当易振荡。
   建议: A 之后的自然扩展, 给操作员真正的力反馈手感。

正交建议(无论选哪个都该上):
- Follower-side deadman + freeze-on-release: 脚踏开关 / 把手按压门控 teleop loop, 主臂掉电或松手即冻结 Franka, 不要让 follower 追着主臂坠下。成本 20-50 USD, 1 天实现, 是最高 ROI 的安全网。

needs_user: 推荐给 Zheng Yuhang 的下一步决策
1. 拍照 / 列清本实验室 Franka-Gello 每关节的 Dynamixel 型号 (5 分钟硬件检查) — 决定 A/E 是否可行。
2. 双向力反馈是硬需求还是 nice-to-have? — 决定要不要走 D 或 E。
3. 缺失的 configs/yam_gello_factr_*.yaml 是在其它内部仓库还是需要从零写? — 决定 A 的工作量。
4. 12k FACTR 设备的预算是否真有? — 决定 D 是否上桌。

推荐顺序: 先做正交的 follower deadman (本周即可); 再确认伺服扭矩后跑 A; 必要时叠加 B 弹簧; 最后才考虑 E/D。

comments.author: Result
comments.body:
DREAMZERO FEASIBILITY (preliminary, before any GPU attempt):

Upstream README dreamzero/README.md explicitly states:
- 'Hardware: Multi-GPU setup (tested on GB200, H100)'
- 'Minimum: 2 GPUs for distributed inference'
- 'CUDA: Compatible GPU with CUDA 12.9+'
- Inference invocation: 'python -m torch.distributed.run --standalone --nproc_per_node=2 socket_test_optimized_AR.py'
- DiT inference latency: ~0.6s on GB200, ~3s on H100; no single-GPU baseline reported.

Backbone is 14B params (Wan2.2-I2V). On bf16: weights alone ~28GB. Even with t5_cpu+vae_cpu offloading, single 32GB RTX 5090 cannot host the DiT for distributed inference because the upstream code requires nproc_per_node>=2 and dim-parallel sequence sharding. Without modifying the torchrun entrypoint to single-rank (which would also bypass the sequence-parallel layers), the server won't start.

ENV mismatch additional: dreamzero pyproject pins torch==2.8.0/torchvision==0.23.0 with cu129; wmfactory env is torch 2.9.0+cu128. Conflict resolvable with a separate env, but moot if hardware blocks anyway.

CONCLUSION for DreamZero in 'worth entering training or modification?' framing:
- Not testable as-published on this single-5090 box.
- If access to multi-GPU box (>=2 H100 or 1 GB200): worth running; the model card claims #1 on MolmoSpaces + RoboArena, trained from scratch on DROID only.
- Alternative: hosted API access via Google form https://forms.gle/zCj5zjDvHsoeuMXU7 (request needed) — could evaluate via sim-evals without local GPU. Mark needs_user if you want me to file that form OR provision a 2-GPU box.

Ctrl-World inference is feasible here (smaller SVD-based, will run after the 8.6G ckpt download finishes ~5min). Will follow with run results.

- Ctrl-World inference is FEASIBLE on single 5090 and produces coherent action-conditioned video.
- Code quality: clean SVD-based pipeline, easy to read; uses standard diffusers; not a hidden-internals monolith. Forkable.
- Architecture relevance to UMI eye-in-hand bimanual: single-camera, 7-DoF cartesian action conditioning -> not directly bimanual, but the AdaLN-cond + SVD spatiotemporal UNet pattern transfers; wrist-camera trajectory == action conditioning matches our framing.
- Worth advancing to training/modification: YES for Ctrl-World. The 95k-traj DROID pretraining baseline is a strong starting point; modify the action encoder to accept dual-arm 16-D and the UNet input to stack left+right wrist views.
- DreamZero: NOT testable here (see prior blocker comment) — needs >=2 H100/GB200. Recommend deferring DreamZero to a multi-GPU box or hosted API; do NOT block UMI ICLR sprint on it.

tasks.title: Build agentic Real2Sim2Real-VLA evaluation pipeline
tasks.description: Construct an agentic AI-powered Real2Sim2Real evaluation pipeline that starts from real-robot traces and failures, builds or mutates simulation tasks/assets, evaluates VLA models in simulation, diagnoses failures, versions traces, and returns to real-robot verification.

comments.author: Codex
comments.body: Meeting sketch suggests a simple operating loop: VLA / robot executes tasks -> agentic eval discovers true failures and sim-real gaps -> Trace-to-Memory turns failures into reusable experience -> Eval-Gated Workflow generates improvements and evaluates them -> World Model screens candidates in imagination -> new agents / workflows / strategies are promoted into harder self-evolving VLA evaluations. Frame this as Agentic REAL2SIM2REAL-VLA Evaluation, not only Sim2Real: real-robot traces and failures -> simulation task/asset reconstruction or mutation -> VLA evaluation in simulation -> diagnosis/versioning/governance -> real-robot verification.

comments.author: Codex
comments.body: Meeting update: track this project as an Alchedata collaboration around runtime-governed Robot-for-Robot data and evaluation. Also keep Periodic Labs as an external reference / competitive signal: their public framing is AI scientist + autonomous labs, where agents act through real experiments, learn from negative results, and use physical science as a verifiable environment. The dashboard framing should borrow the closed-loop lab automation idea but keep our concrete focus on robotics Real2Sim2Real-VLA evaluation.

comments.author: Codex
comments.body: Meeting reference: use DreamDojo (arXiv:2602.06949) as a Real2Sim2Real world-model reference. Relevant ideas: large-scale egocentric human video pretraining for interaction dynamics, continuous latent actions as proxy actions when robot action labels are missing, small-scale robot post-training for target embodiments, and downstream use in live teleoperation, policy evaluation, and model-based planning. For our pipeline, DreamDojo helps motivate a world-model screening layer before real-robot verification.

comments.author: Codex
comments.body: Meeting screenshot update: organize the project around five necessary self-improving robotics functions. 1) Agentic Real2Sim2Real-VLA Evaluation: know whether VLA works on real robots, not only sim scores. 2) Trace-to-Memory Distillation: convert failures from raw logs into transferable experience. 3) Eval-Gated Workflow Evolution: make experience change future agent behavior, not just produce summaries. 4) World-Model-Guided Self-Improvement: screen candidate improvements before costly / risky real-robot trials. 5) Self-Evolving VLA Evaluation: prevent overfitting static benchmarks by generating harder diagnostic tests. Overall loop: evaluate reality -> learn from experience -> improve the system.

Reference object = print-in-place butt hinge (60x40x3mm leaves, 0.4mm clearance) defined in reference/object_spec.md; canonical mesh + iso PNG generated by reference/build_reference.py. Scoring rubric (4 dimensions, weighted /21) in RUBRIC.md. Articraft-style programmatic route fully runnable end-to-end: sdk.py (~450 lines: ArticulatedObject/Part/Articulation/Box/Cylinder/Sphere/hinge + TestContext + URDF/STL/GLB export) -> build_hinge.py -> run.py produces meshes/leaf_a.stl + meshes/leaf_b.stl + meshes/pin.stl + butt_hinge.urdf (2 joints: revolute hinge_axis with axes+limits + fixed pin_to_leaf_a) + butt_hinge.manifest.json + validation_report.json (3 watertight, closest approach 0.400mm, 0 overlap). pytest tests.py = 6/6 pass. scripts/score_mesh.py --route articraft_programmatic = 21/21 (geometry 3 bbox-err 0.000, printability 3 min wall 2.59mm, editability 3 code-driven, metadata 3 URDF+manifest+axes+limits).

comments.author: Progress
comments.body: Commercial routes (Tripo AI 3.1 / Hunyuan 3D 3.1 / Meshy 6): capability sheets in engines/<engine>/capability.md (formats, segmentation, rigging, PoD-relevant gaps); runnable upload skeletons in engines/<engine>/upload.py read API keys from env (TRIPO_API_KEY / REPLICATE_API_TOKEN / MESHY_API_KEY), submit reference/butt_hinge_iso.png byte-identical, poll, download into engines/<engine>/out/. They each exit 2 (needs_input) when the key is missing. None of the three emits URDF or kinematic-chain metadata for a mechanical (non-character) object - this is the structural reason the Articraft-style route wins on the PoD axis. Expected-from-spec scores: Hunyuan 12.5, Meshy 11.0, Tripo 10.5; Articraft measured 21.0. Recommendation in results/comparison.md: programmatic SDK = primary asset layer; commercial mesh generators = visual proposal step that the LLM re-implements in code. To populate the 'measured' columns for the three commercial engines, set the API key env var and re-run scripts/score_mesh.py --route <slug>.

tasks.title: Write Image-Layered World Model survey notes
tasks.description: Define persistent scene / object-contact / robot-ego layers and connect WEM-style world-ego decomposition to IDM, reward model, and augmentation.

comments.author: Codex
comments.body: WEM is a strong conceptual neighbor: it separates persistent, instruction-agnostic world evolution from instruction-conditioned robot ego dynamics for long-horizon navigation-manipulation rollout. For this project, keep the more manipulation-specific split: persistent scene, object/contact transition, and robot/ego action dynamics. The object/contact layer is the important bridge WEM does not isolate explicitly enough for manipulation.

comments.author: Codex
comments.body: Research framing update: the IDM story can be told through Learning from Demonstration / Learning from Observation. Offline IDM recovers missing actions from observation-only videos or adjacent visual states, turning human videos into state-action supervision. If this action recovery is made low-latency and kept in the human feedback loop, it becomes vision-based teleoperation / shared control: the operator shows or intends the next visual state, and the IDM / retargeting layer immediately converts that into robot commands while logging demonstrations for later policy learning.

tasks.title: Build classical retiming baselines
tasks.description: Test TOPPRA / Ruckig-style retiming under velocity, acceleration, jerk, torque, and contact constraints before adding learned timing; references: https://arxiv.org/abs/1707.07239 and https://arxiv.org/abs/2105.04830.

comments.author: Progress
comments.body: Retiming survey complete. Artifact: ~/workspace/superhuman-rate-curve-teleop/task_rate_curve_retiming.md (215 lines). Key framing: TOPPRA and Ruckig are not competitors but different layers — TOPPRA is offline path parameterization (consumes a path + global limits, emits time-optimal velocity profile under velocity/accel/torque/contact), Ruckig is online per-tick OTG (consumes current+target state, emits jerk-limited transition). Constraint coverage table shows TOPPRA owns torque+contact offline, Ruckig owns jerk+real-time. v1 decision: adopt BOTH — TOPPRA for offline retiming-projection of rate-scheduler intent, Ruckig for online jerk-limited transitions on event-predicate changes. Learned timing deferred until classical baseline is measured (verifier plan §7 specifies pass/fail thresholds for feasibility, jerk ratio, extra time cost). Sources: arXiv:1707.07239 (TOPPRA), arXiv:2105.04830 (Ruckig).

Comparison table across 7 axes. v1 composition: T3∘T4∘T1 (sharp two-rate base + perception-hold overlay + gated ramp re-entry); switch to T2 once d_contact estimator is reliable; T5 behind feature flag until SFT/RL story (task_rate_curve_objective_split + task_rate_curve_rl) lands. Verifier plan §7 with concrete pass criteria (T0 must lose on contact, T2 within +10% time vs T1 on free-space, T3 zero completion failures added, T4 zero force overshoot, T5 ≥10% time improvement with no safety regression). 4 open questions feeding event_constraints + SFT-frequency + RL tasks.

tasks.title: Mark event constraints in teleop demos
tasks.description: Do not accelerate perception waits, grasp confirmation, contact settling, or object state transitions blindly.

comments.author: Progress
comments.body: Event constraints inventory complete. Artifact: ~/workspace/superhuman-rate-curve-teleop/task_rate_curve_event_constraints.md (195 lines). 4 events fully operationalized — E1 perception wait (predicate: awaiting reply OR stale beyond τ_perception_stale; hold hard), E2 grasp confirmation (force ≥ f_grasp_min ∧ tactile area ≥ A_grasp_min ∧ optional vision OK for τ_grasp_stable; hold hard; timeout to retry then needs_user after N=3), E3 contact settling (force+tactile rolling-window variance below threshold for τ_stable; soft slowdown, then T4 ramp resumes), E4 object state transition (event labeler emits transition_in_progress/complete/aborted; hold hard; aborts escalate). Composition: hold = OR over all four; ds/dt = 0 if hold else T1/T2/T4 result; hysteretic, with timeouts that escalate not auto-clear. Detection plumbing classified tier-A (deployable v1: E1 E2 E3 on Franka/Cobot-Magic/Piper-X/Linker-FFG) vs tier-B (E4 behind feature flag until FSM or learned labeler ships). Verifier plan §7 with adversarial sensor-dropout tests. Key safety invariant: speed reward only computed when hold=False, so RL cannot learn to short-circuit predicates. 4 open questions on per-embodiment threshold calibration + labeler choice + clipping integration + per-task overrides.

tasks.description: DAgger collects correction data and SFT improves stability / safety; RL starts from that policy and adds speed rewards from velocity / completion time plus smoothness rewards from acceleration and jerk.

comments.author: Progress
comments.body: Artifact: ~/workspace/superhuman-rate-curve-teleop/task_rate_curve_objective_split.md (190 lines). Three-stage pipeline: DAgger (correction data; triggered by event predicates so corrections are captured at the right moments; ends when correction_rate < 0.05 for 20 rollouts), SFT (BC over phase-decoupled action chunks with hold-aware masking so model doesn't learn to drive through perception waits/grasp/settling/object transitions), RL (KL-anchored fine-tune on top with reward = w_speed·R_speed + w_smooth·R_smooth + w_safety·R_safety + w_KL·R_KL). KEY INVARIANT (reward gating table): R_speed is gated on hold=False — RL cannot earn speed reward by short-circuiting event predicates. KEY INVARIANT (handoff): DAgger never sees speed reward; SFT loss is hold-aware; RL starts from π_SFT not scratch; event predicates read by all stages; Ruckig is in the action path during RL rollouts so jerk-limit violations are infeasible by construction. 4 risks documented (DAgger plateau, SFT pace overfit, reward gating bypass via sensor dropout, action-chunk RL interface). Pass criteria per stage in §7; if a stage fails, the next does not start.

tasks.title: Ablate SFT action sampling frequency
tasks.description: Train SFT variants with different action sampling frequencies / downsampling ratios, then compare execution speed, action continuity, jerk, and success stability before RL.

comments.author: Artifact
comments.body: Artifact: ~/workspace/superhuman-rate-curve-teleop/task_rate_curve_sft_action_frequency.md (186 lines). DESIGN-ONLY — no fake numbers, no runs yet. Ablation grid: 8 SFT variants = 4 sampling rates {5, 20, 50, 100} Hz × 2 chunking modes {single-action, K=16 chunk}, all trained from same D_dagger ∪ D_teleop with identical optimizer/seeds (3 seeds final). 8 metrics covering speed (T_complete), safety (success_rate, hold_violation_rate, jerk_ratio_to_limit), smoothness (continuity_L2, jerk_peak measured both pre-Ruckig and post-Ruckig, over_correction_count), and deployability (compute_latency_ms < 1000/f_act). PASS CRITERIA: variant must clear jerk_ratio<1, hold_violation_rate=0, latency budget, success ≥ baseline-0.05. Among passers Pareto-rank on (T_complete, jerk_peak, continuity_L2) and pick closest to human-demo time stats (SFT not allowed to outpace human — that's RL's job). Evaluation protocol mandates split by demo-identity not frame, simulator first with same Ruckig layer as production, real-robot validation on top-2 only. 3 named risks (downsampling artefact, chunk-vs-single confounding, baseline_human_success definition) with mitigations. 3 open questions on N_eval sizing, frequency-conditioned variant, real-robot wear budget.

tasks.title: Run DAgger -> SFT -> RL pilot
tasks.description: Use force / tactile signals to detect unsafe or unstable rollouts and request correction, train the SFT base, then RL-tune only smoother and faster execution; compare operation types to see which benefit from acceleration.

comments.author: Artifact
comments.body: Artifact: ~/workspace/superhuman-rate-curve-teleop/task_rate_curve_rl.md (260 lines). PLAN ONLY — explicit non-claims in §12: no model trained, no DAgger run, no empirical numbers. Pilot scope: 3 op types spanning acceleration-benefit spectrum (free-space reach, pick-place with contact, precision insertion). Safety detector with 5 rules R1..R5 (force threshold, tactile area Δ, slip via tactile-vs-path-tangent, contact-mode inconsistency vs E3 predicate, impedance residual). DAgger termination: correction_rate<0.05 over 20 rollouts AND zero un-corrected safety incidents. SFT stage gates on the criteria from objective_split §7. RL: KL-regularized policy gradient with reward weights w_speed=1.0, w_smooth=0.3, w_safety=10.0, w_KL=0.1; 2-axis sweep on (w_smooth, w_KL). Per-op-type targets: free-space ≥30% T_complete + ≥20% jerk reduction; pick-place ≥15% T_complete + ≥20% jerk; insertion 0% speed (bounded by perception) + ≥30% jerk. Headline output (§6) is op-type benefit comparison TABLE — TBD cells make explicit which numbers are not measured. Verifier §7 specifies CSV/JSON logs + validity criteria (no untrapped safety incidents, KL bound holding, termination reason recorded). 5 risks documented including DAgger expense on hardware, safety-detector false positives, op-type generalization.

comments.author: Progress
comments.body: [Codex route 2026-05-23] Route: begin offline with one teleop demo and a phase-aware playback-rate curve. Compare naive time scaling, TOPP-RA/Ruckig-style retiming, and learned/SFT frequency variants before any real-robot speedup. Report success proxy, jerk/acceleration, event-guardrail violations, and contact-risk intervals.

tasks.title: 比较 classical retiming 与 SFT action-frequency baseline
tasks.description: Why now: 下一步要知道速度提升来自轨迹 retiming 还是 policy frequency/behavior learning。Deliverable: TOPPRA/Ruckig-style retiming baseline 与 SFT action-frequency ablation 的实验设计或 mock implementation。Acceptance: 同一 demo 上输出速度/连续性/稳定性/jerk 对比。

tasks.title: 定义加速执行的事件/安全 guardrails
tasks.description: Why now: 不能盲目加速 perception waits、grasp confirmation、contact settling。Deliverable: event constraints schema + runtime checker。Acceptance: checker 能对一条 proposed speed curve 标出不可加速区间、force/tactile guardrail 和 fail reason。
